revision.text:
'''西田長男'''（にしだ ながお、[[1909年]][[3月31日]]－[[1981年]][[3月28日]]）は、神道学者、[[國學院大學]]名誉教授。 三重県出身。國學院大学卒。[[宮地直一]]に師事。戦前は[[大倉精神文化研究所]]研究員、[[東京帝国大学]]講師、戦後は国学院大学教授。1955年「日本宗教の発生に関する一試論」で國學院大[[文学博士]]。
==著書== *『[[葉隠]]講話』有精堂出版部 1942 *『神道史の研究』雄山閣 1943 *『神道論』躬行会・大倉精神文化研究所紀要 1943 *『神々と国家』明世堂 1944 *『日本古典の史的研究』理想社 1956 *『日本宗教思想史の研究』理想社 1956 *『神道史の研究 第2』理想社 1957 *『古代文学の周辺』南雲堂桜楓社 1964 *『神社の歴史的研究』塙書房 1966 *『日本神道史研究』全10巻 講談社、1978-79 :第1巻 (総論編) :第2-3巻 古代編 :第4-5巻 中世編 :第6-7巻 近世編 :第8-9巻 神社編 :第10巻 (古典編)
==共著== *『神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて』[[高橋秀元]]共著 工作舎 1980 プラネタリー・ブックス *『神々の原影』[[三橋健]]共著 平河出版社 1983
==校訂== *[[大山為起]]『氏族母鑑 22巻』伏見稲荷大社 1957 *『[[中臣秡]]・中臣秡抄』叢文社 1977 吉田叢書
==記念論集== *『神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集』国学院大学神道史学会編 名著普及会 1987
==参考== *デジタル版日本人名大辞典:[http://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7]
{{デフォルトソート:にしたなかお}} [[Category:神道学者]] [[Category:國學院大學の教員]]

'''西田長男'''（にしだ ながお、[[1909年]][[3月31日]]－[[1981年]][[3月28日]]）は、[[神道]]学者、[[國學院大學]]名誉[[教授]]。 [[三重県]]出身。國學院大学卒。[[宮地直一]]に師事。戦前は[[大倉精神文化研究所]]研究員、[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]講師、戦後は国学院大学教授。1955年「日本宗教の発生に関する一試論」で國學院大[[文学博士]]。

==共著== *『神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて』[[高橋秀元]]共著 [[工作舎]] 1980 プラネタリー・ブックス *『神々の原影』[[三橋健]]共著 [[平河出版社]] 1983
==校訂== *[[大山為起]]『氏族母鑑 22巻』[[伏見稲荷大社]] 1957 *『[[中臣秡]]・中臣秡抄』叢文社 1977 吉田叢書
==記念論集== *『神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集』国学院大学神道史学会編 名著普及会 1987

revision.comment: Created page with " '''Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集) is a practical and specialized divination text traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei ({{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}). However, it is believed to have been compiled after Seimei's death, with various theories regarding its date of origin. The work is also referred to as '''Sangoku Sōden Senmyōrekikyōchū''' (三国相伝宣明暦経註)..."
revision.text:
'''Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集) is a practical and specialized divination text traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei ({{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}). However, it is believed to have been compiled after Seimei's death, with various theories regarding its date of origin.
The work is also referred to as '''Sangoku Sōden Senmyōrekikyōchū''' (三国相伝宣明暦経註), abbreviated as '''Hokinaiden''' (簠簋内伝), '''Hoki''' (簠簋), or '''Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (金烏玉兎集).
The term "簠 (Hoki)" and "簋 (Gui)" refer to ancient Chinese ritual vessels.
== Overview == The {{ill|Kin'u|ja|金烏}}, a three-legged golden crow associated with the sun, is a mythical bird symbolizing the sun, while the {{ill|Gyokuto|ja|玉兎}}, a rabbit said to dwell on the moon, represents the moon. These symbols reflect the content of this secret manual of onmyōji, which focuses on understanding the circulation of energy and divination based on the sun and moon's movements.
== Authorship == Although the text is traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei, it was likely compiled after his death, casting doubt on this claim.
In the mid-Edo period text *Taizan-shū*, Abe no Yasufuku ({{ill|Abe no Yasufuku|ja|土御門泰福}}), the head of the Abe clan's onmyōdō school, is quoted as saying, "Hokinaiden was created by a Shingon monk and is not part of the Abe family tradition. Seimei passed down the astronomy brought back from Tang China by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}."
According to *Nihon Onmyōdō-shi Sōsetsu* (A General Overview of Japanese Onmyōdō), {{ill|Murayama Shūichi|ja|村山修一}} speculates, based on explanations by {{ill|Nishida Osamu|ja|西田長男}}, that the author may have been a shrine priest at Gion Shrine and a descendant of Seimei. After Abe no Yoshihira, Seimei's descendants split into three lines: Toki, Enya, and {{ill|Abe no Yasuchika|ja|安倍泰親}}. It is suggested that a descendant of Enya who joined Gion Shrine, specifically Seichō, might be the author of *Hokinaiden*.
However, {{ill|Nakamura Shōhachi|ja|中村璋八}}, who conducted a comprehensive study of the text, states that there is no definitive evidence supporting Seichō as the author, leaving the authorship unresolved.
== Structure == The text consists of five volumes: * **Volume 1**: Explains the origins of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and various {{ill|directional deities|ja|方位神}} and their auspicious and inauspicious influences. * **Volume 2**: Details the origins of the primordial deity {{ill|Pangu|ja|盤古}} and his progeny, as well as calendar-based divinations. * **Volume 3**: Discusses additional elements such as {{ill|Nayin|ja|納音}} and {{ill|Kūbō|ja|空亡}}, not covered in the previous volumes. * **Volume 4**: Focuses on {{ill|Feng Shui|ja|風水}} and architectural auspices. * **Volume 5**: Covers {{ill|Esoteric Buddhist astrology|ja|密教占星術}}, specifically {{ill|Shuku-yo|ja|宿曜占術}}.
{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月}}
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4}}</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|ja|文殊菩薩}} in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}
== External Links == * [http://dl.ndl.go.jp/search/searchResult?featureCode=all&searchWord=三國相傳陰陽 National Diet Library listings of *Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū*] * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200019239/ *Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū* - Joint Usage Center for Humanities Open Data] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520572357557459072 Religious World of *Hokinaiden*] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1572543026800934912 *Hokinaiden* Notes: Relation to Gozu Tennō Legends]
{{Book-stub}}

== Structure == The text consists of five volumes: * '''Volume 1''': Explains the origins of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] and various {{ill|directional deities|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:方位神]] and their auspicious and inauspicious influences. * '''Volume 2''': Details the origins of the primordial deity {{ill|Pangu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:盤古]] and his progeny, as well as calendar-based divinations. * '''Volume 3''': Discusses additional elements such as {{ill|Nayin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:納音]] and {{ill|Kūbō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:空亡]], not covered in the previous volumes. * '''Volume 4''': Focuses on {{ill|Feng Shui|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:風水]] and architectural auspices. * '''Volume 5''': Covers {{ill|Esoteric Buddhist astrology|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]], specifically {{ill|Shuku-yo|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:宿曜占術]].
{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月]]
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4]]</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.

== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4]]</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:祇園信仰]] * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:八坂神社]]

[[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集]] [[category:Shinnaiden forks]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]]

'''Sangoku Sōden Onmy''' by {{ill|Nishida Osamu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:西田長男]], that the author may have been a shrine priest at Gion Shrine and a descendant of Seimei. After Abe no Yoshihira, Seimei's descendants split into three lines: Toki, Enya, and {{ill|Abe no Yasuchika|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:安倍泰親]]. It is suggested that a descendant of Enya who joined Gion Shrine, specifically Seichō, might be the author of *Hokinaiden*.
However, {{ill|Nakamura Shōhachi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]], who conducted a comprehensive study of the text, states that there is no definitive evidence supporting Seichō as the author, leaving the authorship unresolved.

== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist]]

[[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[Category:Shinnaiden]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集]] [[category:Shinnaiden forks]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]]

'''Sangoku Sōden Onmy''' by {{ill|Nishida Osamu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:西田長男]], that the author may have been a shrine priest at Gion Shrine and a descendant of Seimei. After Abe no Yoshihira, Seimei's descendants split into three lines: Toki, Enya, and {{ill|Abe no Yasuchika|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:安倍泰親]]. It is suggested that a descendant of Enya who joined Gion Shrine, specifically Seichō, might be the author of *Hokinaiden*.
However, {{ill|Nakamura Shōhachi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]], who conducted a comprehensive study of the text, states that there is no definitive evidence supporting Seichō as the author, leaving the authorship unresolved.
== Structure == The text consists of five volumes: * '''Volume 1''': Explains the origins of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] and various {{ill|directional deities|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:方位神]] and their auspicious and inauspicious influences. * '''Volume 2''': Details the origins of the primordial deity {{ill|Pangu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:盤古]] and his progeny, as well as calendar-based divinations. * '''Volume 3''': Discusses additional elements such as {{ill|Nayin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:納音]] and {{ill|Kūbō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:空亡]], not covered in the previous volumes. * '''Volume 4''': Focuses on {{ill|Feng Shui|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:風水]] and architectural auspices. * '''Volume 5''': Covers {{ill|Esoteric Buddhist astrology|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]], specifically {{ill|Shuku-yo|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:宿曜占術]].
{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月]]
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4]]</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.

'''Sangoku Sōden Onmy''' by {{ill|Nishida Osamu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:西田長男]], that the author may have been a shrine priest at Gion Shrine and a descendant of Seimei. After Abe no Yoshihira, Seimei's descendants split into three lines: Toki, Enya, and {{ill|Abe no Yasuchika|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:安倍泰親]]. It is suggested that a descendant of Enya who joined Gion Shrine, specifically Seichō, might be the author of *Hokinaiden*.
However, {{ill|Nakamura Shōhachi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]], who conducted a comprehensive study of the text, states that there is no definitive evidence supporting Seichō as the author, leaving the authorship unresolved.
== Structure == The text consists of five volumes: * '''Volume 1''': Explains the origins of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] and various {{ill|directional deities|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:方位神]] and their auspicious and inauspicious influences. * '''Volume 2''': Details the origins of the primordial deity {{ill|Pangu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:盤古]] and his progeny, as well as calendar-based divinations. * '''Volume 3''': Discusses additional elements such as {{ill|Nayin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:納音]] and {{ill|Kūbō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:空亡]], not covered in the previous volumes. * '''Volume 4''': Focuses on {{ill|Feng Shui|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:風水]] and architectural auspices. * '''Volume 5''': Covers {{ill|Esoteric Buddhist astrology|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]], specifically {{ill|Shuku-yo|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:宿曜占術]].
{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月]]
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4]]</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:祇園信仰]] * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nishida Osamu-qq]] [[ja:八坂神社]]
== External Links == * [http://dl.ndl.go.jp/search/searchResult?featureCode=all&searchWord=三國相傳陰陽 National Diet Library listings of *Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū*] * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200019239/ *Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū* - Joint Usage Center for Humanities Open Data] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520572357557459072 Religious World of *Hokinaiden*] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1572543026800934912 *Hokinaiden* Notes: Relation to Gozu Tennō Legends]

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909年]][[3月31日]] – [[1981年]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909年]][[3月31日]] – [[1981年]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909年]][[3月31日]] – [[1981年]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909年]][[3月31日]] – [[1981年]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909年]][[3月31日]] – [[1981年]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987

revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=draft}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=draft}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=draft}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=draft}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=draft}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=draft}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=draft}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=draft}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=draft}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=draft}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=draft}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=draft}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=draft}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=draft}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|12=draft}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|12=draft}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=draft}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|12=draft}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987

'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=draft}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=draft}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=draft}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=draft}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=draft}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=draft}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=draft}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=draft}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=draft}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=draft}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=draft}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=draft}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=draft}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=draft}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=draft}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|12=draft}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|12=draft}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=draft}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|12=draft}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

revision.text:
'''西田 長男'''（にしだ ながお、[[1909年]][[3月31日]] - [[1981年]][[3月28日]]）は、日本の[[神道]]学者、[[國學院大學]]名誉[[教授]]。
[[三重県]]出身。國學院大學卒。[[宮地直一]]に師事。戦前は[[大倉精神文化研究所]]研究員、[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]講師、戦後は國學院大學教授。1955年「日本宗教の発生に関する一試論」で國學院大[[文学博士]]。
== 著書 == *『[[葉隠]]講話』有精堂出版部 1942 *『神道史の研究』[[雄山閣]] 1943 *『神道論』躬行会・大倉精神文化研究所紀要 1943 *『神々と国家』[[明世堂]] 1944 *『日本古典の史的研究』[[理想社]] 1956 *『日本宗教思想史の研究』理想社 1956 *『神道史の研究 第2』理想社 1957 *『古代文学の周辺』[[南雲堂]][[桜楓社]] 1964 *『[[神社]]の歴史的研究』[[塙書房]] 1966 *『日本神道史研究』全10巻 [[講談社]]、1978-79 :第1巻 (総論編) :第2-3巻 古代編 :第4-5巻 中世編 :第6-7巻 近世編 :第8-9巻 神社編 :第10巻 (古典編)
== 共著 == *『神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて』[[高橋秀元]]共著 [[工作舎]] 1980 プラネタリー・ブックス *『神々の原影』[[三橋健 (神道学者)|三橋健]]共著 [[平河出版社]] 1983
== 校訂 == *[[大山為起]]『氏族母鑑 22巻』[[伏見稲荷大社]] 1957 *『[[中臣秡]]・中臣秡抄』叢文社 1977 吉田叢書
== 記念論集 == *『神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集』国学院大学神道史学会編 名著普及会 1987
== 参考 == * デジタル版日本人名大辞典:[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}
{{デフォルトソート:にした なかお}} [[Category:神道学者]] [[Category:國學院大學の教員]] [[Category:國學院大學出身の人物]] [[Category:三重県出身の人物]] [[Category:1909年生]] [[Category:1981年没]]

== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Kokugakuin University Alumni]]
revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor|qq=}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|ja|教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor|qq=}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|qq=}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|qq=}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Taisha|qq=}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|qq=}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|qq=}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Faith|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://dl.ndl.go.jp/search/searchResult?featureCode=all&searchWord=三國相傳陰陽 National Diet Library listings of *Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū*] * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200019239/ *Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū* - Joint Usage Center for Humanities Open Data] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520572357557459072 Religious World of *Hokinaiden*] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1572543026800934912 *Hokinaiden* Notes: Relation to Gozu Tennō Legends]
{{translated page|ja|西田長男|version=94713117|comment=Imported full ja history}}

A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Kokugakuin University alumni
revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|qq=}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|qq=}}'' – [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|qq=}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|qq=}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
{{Normdaten}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Faith|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://dl.ndl.go.jp/search/searchResult?featureCode=all&searchWord=三國相傳陰陽 National Diet Library listings of *Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū*] * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200019239/ *Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū* - Joint Usage Center for Humanities Open Data] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520572357557459072 Religious World of *Hokinaiden*] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1572543026800934912 *Hokinaiden* Notes: Relation to Gozu Tennō Legends]
{{translated page|ja|西田長男|version=94713117|comment=Imported full ja history}}
