'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|qq=}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|qq=}}'' – [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|qq=}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|qq=}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Faith|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Japanese Pseudepigrapha
revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Faith|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Shintō Texts
revision.text:
'''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|qq=}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|qq=}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|qq=|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics

A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, [[:en:1909|1909]][[3月31日]] – [[:en:1981|1981]][[3月28日]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] scholar and Honorary [[:en:Professor|Professor]] at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]].
A native of [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], he graduated from [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]] and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.

Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at [[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|1=Hideharu Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922493}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|1=Mitsuhashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356006}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629296]] Wikidata name: Nagao Nishida [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}}[[3月31日]] – {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}}[[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|en|Professor|WD=Q121594|lt=Professor}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|1=Hideharu Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922493}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|1=Mitsuhashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356006}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=西田長男}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}}[[3月31日]] – {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}}[[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|en|Professor|WD=Q121594|lt=Professor}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|1=Hideharu Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922493}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|1=Mitsuhashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356006}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983
== Edited Works == * Edited {{ill|Ooyama Tameki|ja|大山為起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyama Tameki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s ''{{ill|Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja|氏族母鑑 22巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku Mokan, Vol. 22|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, 1957 * ''{{ill|Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja|中臣秡・中臣秡抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Momo / Nakatomi Momo-shō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491610}}'' – Sōbunsha, 1977 (Yoshida Sōsho)
== Commemorative Essay Collection == * ''{{ill|Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja|神道及び神道史 西田長男博士追悼論文集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Collected Essays in Memory of Dr. Nishida Nagao on Shinto and Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Edited by the Kokugakuin University Shinto History Society, Meicho Fukyūkai, 1987
== References == * Digital version of the Japanese Biographical Dictionary: [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%A5%BF%E7%94%B0%E9%95%B7%E7%94%B7-1099103]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|native_name=西田長男}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}}[[3月31日]] – {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}[[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|en|Professor|WD=Q121594|lt=Professor|1=Professor}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|1=Hideharu Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922493}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|1=Q11356006|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356006}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西田長男]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|native_name=西田長男}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}}[[3月31日]] – {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}[[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|en|Professor|WD=Q121594|lt=Professor|1=Professor}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo, 1943 * ''{{ill|Gods and the State|ja|神々と国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gods and the State|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Meiseidō, 1944 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3: Ancient Period : Volumes 4–5: Medieval Period : Volumes 6–7: Early Modern Period : Volumes 8–9: Studies on Shrines : Volume 10: Classics
== Co-authored Works == * ''{{ill|The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja|神道の宇宙 魂のコスモグラムに遊星的郷愁を求めて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Cosmic Aspect of Shinto: Seeking Planetary Nostalgia in the Cosmogram of the Soul|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Takahashi Hidemoto|ja|高橋秀元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hidemoto|1=Hideharu Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922493}}, Kōsōsha, 1980 (Planetary Books) * ''{{ill|The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja|神々の原影|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Original Shadows of the Gods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – co-authored with {{ill|Mitsuhashi Ken|ja|三橋健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhashi Ken|1=Q11356006|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356006}}, Heikawa Shuppan, 1983

{{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1909|3|31}}|death_date={{death date|1981|3|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|native_name=西田長男}} '''Nishida Nagao''' (にしだ ながお, {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}}[[3月31日]] – {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}[[3月28日]]) was a Japanese {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} scholar and Honorary {{ill|Professor|en|Professor|WD=Q121594|lt=Professor|1=Professor}} at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}.
A native of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, he graduated from {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} and studied under {{ill|Miyaji Naoki|ja|宮地直一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji Naoki|1=Naokazu Miyaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453535}}. Before World War II, he worked as a researcher at the {{ill|Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Seishin Bunka Kenkyūjo|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}} and as a lecturer at {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}; after the war, he served as a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}. In 1955, with his paper “A Preliminary Discussion on the Origin of Japanese Religions” (『日本宗教の発生に関する一試論』), he was awarded a Doctorate in Literature by Kokugakuin University.
== Works == * ''{{ill|Hagakure Lectures|ja|葉隠講話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagakure Lectures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Yūseido Publishing, 1942 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History|ja|神道史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – {{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}, 1943 * ''{{ill|On Shinto Theory|ja|神道論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Shinto Theory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Bulletin of the Kōkōkai/Ōkura Seishin Classics|ja|日本古典の史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja|日本宗教思想史の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in the History of Japanese Religious Thought|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1956 * ''{{ill|Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja|神道史の研究 第2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Shinto History, Vol. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Risōsha, 1957 * ''{{ill|Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja|古代文学の周辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peripheral Aspects of Ancient Literature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Nanmundō/Sakura Fūsha, 1964 * ''{{ill|Historical Studies of Shrines|ja|神社の歴史的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Studies of Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' – Hanawa Shobō, 1966 * ''{{ill|Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja|日本神道史研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Studies in Japanese Shinto History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (10 volumes) – Kōdansa, 1978–79 : Volume 1: General Introduction : Volumes 2–3:
